title: WIP: CI:DOCS: Add document for libpod release process

comments.body: @mheon when you have a mo.  PTAL https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/6781/files#diff-3545a3a52685c6e1f95c1060b4febcb4R5-R34  I think you mentioned there should be a total of 3 commits in the release PR.  Which one am I missing and where in the sequence should it appear?

It's three commits across two PRs.

Commit 1, PR 1: Update release notes with all changes of note

Wait until that merges, then rebase, making sure that commit is included.

Commit 1, PR 2: Bump to vA.B.C (the actual release, signed)
Commit 2, PR 2: Bump to vA.B.(C+1)-dev (Bump to a development version, so further builds do not show as `vA.B.C` in `podman info` - makes absolutely sure only the release commit is identified as a full release, not a prerelease.)

> It's three commits across two PRs.

It adds a **lot** of complexity for automation running in one PR to (reliably) depend upon previous PR's commits. Also, since we have a merge-bot in play, it's possible a merge (even accidental) can come in out-of-order.  Can the release workflow be changed to use a single PR with:

1. [HEAD^2] Update release notes with all changes of note
2. [HEAD^1] Bump to vA.B.C (the actual release, signed)
3. [HEAD] Dev Release commit Bump to vA.B.(C+1)-dev

Also, it would be far safer (and less complicated), if all the release-important things can be validated in a single release PR (before the automated release).  This workflow would guarantee no other commits can merge between 1 and 3.  Thereby:

> makes absolutely sure only the release commit is identified as a full release, not a prerelease.

Another alternative that comes to mind is:  Have release automation file the `-dev` PR, which can then follow the normal process (except that a human doesn't have to open it).

@cevich I guess a rebase will resolve the flakes
Update: This PR is *on hold* behind #6784 (which will take some time)

I'm late to the party @cevich, but @mheon had put together a google doc a while back.  I'm not sure it's still on point, but thought I'd link you to it in case it's helpful.  https://docs.google.com/document/d/1Yj8B1liKV1lVnqfAcpDByOcB7lYZ5VI4xTLC6Jc6FFc/edit

@TomSweeneyRedHat you're not late, I'm still working my down to this.  It's the last item to be run by the new CI setup I'm working on.  When I get closer, I'm planning on re-re-re-reviewing everything here, and ensuring the doc and my understanding is up to snuff.  So if you have time to make corrections/clarifications here, it will be eventually (much) appreciated :D
Closing this PR for now, I will likely re-open it later on once my pile of other/unrelated CI changes stabilize.
